events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: structural bolting, turn-of-nut match marks absent or not rotated. This is a defect - turn-of-nut method requires match marks and rotation. Absent match marks means cannot verify proper tightening. This is non-compliant with RCSC. Decision: fail. Risk: high (structural connections). Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 80. Confidence high.

"Re-tighten bolts using RCSC turn-of-nut procedure with proper match marks",
"Apply and verify match marks on nut/bolt/steel before and after rotation",
"Request third-party inspection of reworked connection before closeout"

"rationale": "The turn-of-nut method requires visible match marks and a specified rotation from snug-tight to verify pretension. Absent or unrotated match marks mean the connection cannot be confirmed as properly pretensioned, making it non-compliant and defective at rough-complete stage.",
"references": [
"RCSC Specification for Structural Joints Using High-Strength Bolts, Section 8.2 Turn-of-Nut Method"
]
